engineering_practice.rule: In automatic mode, rings/diaphragms are placed at shell-course joints. Joints too close to heads are skipped. Strength calculation may require additional rings.

standards.title: Reservoirs steel horizontal for petroleum products. Technical specifications
standards.role: Base product standard for horizontal steel tanks; refers strength calculations to vessel strength standards.
calculator_use: link from RGS type selection and normative report

standards.title: Vessels and apparatus. Norms and methods of strength calculation
standards.role: Legacy/reference calculation basis for shells under internal and external pressure.
calculator_use: external pressure stability check between stiffeners

standards.title: Vessels and apparatus. Norms and methods of strength calculation. Cylindrical and conical shells, convex and flat bottoms and covers
standards.role: Modern strength calculation line for cylindrical shells and stiffening elements.
calculator_use: future required moment of inertia for stiffening rings

standards.title: Loads and actions
standards.role: General load combinations and environmental load basis.

implementation_notes: Current code checks shell external pressure capacity between rings and uses 1490 mm shell-course layout as the automatic minimum.
Next step is to calculate required ring stiffness/section and compare it with selected profile properties, not only ring count.
